list.name: scan_interval
list.label: scan_interval (s)
list.description: Your scan_interval in seconds (10m=600, 30m=1800, 60m=3600). Drives the 'falling behind' threshold line on the Scan tick duration panel.

list.name: cleanup_interval
list.label: cleanup_interval (s)
list.description: Your cleanup_interval in seconds (1d=86400, 12h=43200). Drives the per-interval window on the Cleanup runs panel — expect ~1 ok run per interval.

panels.title: Scanner — is the scan keeping up?
Scan tick duration (last, per namespace)
panels.description: Wall-clock time of the last scan tick (gauge).

panels.title: Scan ticks (ok vs error, per 1h)
panels.description: Count of scan ticks over a 1h window, split by result. Scans run every scan_interval (10-60m), so this uses increase() over a window >= the interval instead of per-second rate. A nonzero 'error' line is the signal (errors + Temporal RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED = throttling). Widen the window to 6h if scan_interval=60m.

panels.title: Workflow throughput (per tick)
panels.description: Work done by each scan cycle: listed = scanned via Visibility; processed = new or history-changed; updated = written to DB; skipped = unchanged. These are gauges set at the end of each tick (exact per-scan totals, not per-second rate) — one point per tick, held until the next. listed ≈ processed + skipped; the processed/listed ratio shows how much churn each scan finds.

panels.title: Workflows dropped (tokenization errors)
panels.description: Count of workflows skipped because tokenization failed (a new/unhandled event variant would silently lose a workflow). Expected to be flat 0. Any bump = investigate the scanner logs (workflow_id, run_id, error) — the detailed reason lives in logs, not in a metric label (that would blow up cardinality). 'or vector(0)' keeps a green zero line so an empty panel means 'healthy', not 'no data'.

panels.title: History pages & events fetched (per 1h)
panels.description: History-fetch volume — the 'why' behind History fetch duration. events = data pulled, pages = gRPC round-trips. 'events/page' (right axis) is the effective server page size: low ratio + many pages = lots of round-trips → consider raising maximum_page_size in temporal.rs to cut pages and fetch latency. Uses increase() over 1h (not per-second rate) since fetches are bursty every scan_interval.

panels.title: Unique ratio
panels.description: distinct_hashes / total rows — lower = more duplication captured.

panels.title: DB write errors (per 1h)
panels.description: SQLite failed upserts over the last hour. SQLite is single-writer, so an error here = lock contention / busy_timeout exceeded / disk problem. Expected flat 0 ('or vector(0)' keeps a green zero line so empty means healthy, not 'no data'). This is the only place DB-write failures are distinguishable from other tick errors.

panels.title: DB write duration (p50/p99)
panels.description: Per-upsert SQLite write latency (op=upsert only; no namespace — write latency is a property of the shared DB/disk, not any one namespace). Secondary diagnostic: local writes are normally sub-ms, so p50 hugs 0 and only p99 moves — under write contention or a slow disk.

panels.title: Cleanup runs (ok vs error, per interval)
panels.description: Cleanup runs over the last cleanup_interval window, split by result.

panels.title: Cleanup rows deleted (per run)
panels.description: Exact number of rows removed by the last cleanup run (gauge set at end of run_once), per namespace — workflows that left the tracked set (finished/failed/canceled or gone from Temporal).

panels.title: Cleanup duration (last run)
panels.description: Wall-clock time of the last cleanup run (gauge), per namespace. Cleanup runs ~once per cleanup_interval (default 1d), so there's no distribution to percentile — you read each run directly. Scales with the number of aged workflows checked (one DescribeWorkflowExecution gRPC call each), so a slow run = large tracked set or Temporal slow on describes. Low-value diagnostic — no threshold (a run is seconds vs a ~1d interval) and no alert; glance at it if cleanup errors or the DB stops shrinking.

panels.title: gRPC calls rate (by operation)
panels.description: SDK counter 'request'; no temporal_ prefix, no _total suffix.

panels.title: gRPC request latency (p50/p99)
panels.description: Buckets in seconds; metric name is request_latency (no _seconds).

panels.title: gRPC long-poll latency (p99)
panels.description: Long-polls are long by nature — tracked separately.

panels.title: FD saturation
panels.description: Open descriptors vs the process limit — leak / exhaustion signal.
